Class PokemonView

java.lang.Object
javafx.application.Application
view.PokemonView
All Implemented Interfaces:
Observer
public class PokemonView extends javafx.application.Application implements Observer
PokemonView builds and contains the logic for the UI. Its split into 3 sections, the Main Menu, Battle scene and Challenges.
Author:
Matt Lagier, Bradley Adams

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class javafx.application.Application

    Description
    private void
    battleUIBattleScreen()
    battleUIBattleScreen builds a 4 quadrant group of containers that contain - Top Left: P2 Health, Top Right: P2 Mon Sprite, Bottom Left: P1 Mon Sprite and Bottom Right: P1 health.
    private void
    battleUIBottomBar()
    battleUIBottomBar creates the bar at the bottom which contains the controls for playing the game.
    private javafx.scene.layout.StackPane
    battleUIPlayerMove()
    Creates the player action section of the bottom bar, and handles button logic for basic actions like attack, run, and pokemon switch.

    Calls necessary calls to create BattleUI Scene

    playerAttackPane builds the pane responsible for showing the current Mons moves, the amount of PP, and attack type.
    private void

    playerMonSelectionPane builds the pane for selecting a mon either when you want to switch it out or when the current one faints.

    • setUpBattle

      private void setUpBattle()
      Sets up the battle

    • battleUITesting

      private javafx.scene.Scene battleUITesting(javafx.stage.Stage stage)
      Calls necessary calls to create BattleUI Scene
      Returns:
      battleScene

    • battleUIBattleScreen

      private void battleUIBattleScreen()
      battleUIBattleScreen builds a 4 quadrant group of containers that contain - Top Left: P2 Health, Top Right: P2 Mon Sprite, Bottom Left: P1 Mon Sprite and Bottom Right: P1 health. Each quadrant is 240x110 pixels big.

    • battleUIBottomBar

      private void battleUIBottomBar()
      battleUIBottomBar creates the bar at the bottom which contains the controls for playing the game. Bottom Bar itself creates the base layer bar and the basic 4 interaction functions which other methods build on-top of.

    • battleUIPlayerMove

      private javafx.scene.layout.StackPane battleUIPlayerMove()
      Creates the player action section of the bottom bar, and handles button logic for basic actions like attack, run, and pokemon switch.
      Returns:

    • playerAttackPane

      private void playerAttackPane()
      playerAttackPane builds the pane responsible for showing the current Mons moves, the amount of PP, and attack type. Selecting an attack then sends it to the controller.

    • playerMonSelectionPane

      private void playerMonSelectionPane()
      playerMonSelectionPane builds the pane for selecting a mon either when you want to switch it out or when the current one faints.

    • update

      public void update(Observable o, Object arg)
      Update the battle UI based on information from the two player objects. Updates include: Team pokemon, health, PP, Moves and move type, pokemon status, and checks for game over states.
      Specified by:
      update in interface Observer
